reasoning: The ISW recently reported that Ukraine has launched counter-offensives between late January and mid-March 2026 that liberated over 400 square kilometres in the Oleksandrivka and Hulyaipole directions. While Russian forces continue to progress slowly at roughly 5.5 square kilometres per day (translating to ~165 sq km/month), a sudden Ukrainian push of a couple hundred square kilometres within a single calendar month could easily outpace Russian gains. Although the first three months of 2026 were generally assessed as net Russian gains when averaged over the entire quarter, localized counterattacks offer strong potential for Ukraine to have at least one net positive month. Ukraine's strategy of targeted offensives while wearing down Russian attacks makes a net-positive month relatively likely before the end of the year, supporting a 70% probability estimate.
